keywords.name: mass-scale
keywords.description: For partial mass density scaling, set the desired timestep. See below for further discussion.

keywords.name: energy
keywords.description: Toggle energy summation. All energy calculations are incremental, except strain energies from the block insitu stress command (if it is used with energy on ).

keywords.name: stiffness
keywords.description: Set damping for static and dynamic analyses. local damping is the default for this keyword. Use combined damping set to f (the damping value is 0.8 by default). COMBINED DAMPING IS THE DEFAULT for non-dynamic 3DEC models. Set different stiffness damping factors, depending on the material numbers and contact types. See below for further information. The group name of the first group of block(s) in contact. The group name of the second group of block(s) in contact. The slot name of the groups. Face-face contact normal damping factor. Face-face contact shear damping factor. Vertex-vertex contact normal damping factor. Vertex-vertex contact shear damping factor. Vertex-edge contact normal damping factor. Vertex-edge contact shear damping factor. Vertex-face contact normal damping factor. Vertex-face contact shear damping factor. Edge-face contact normal damping factor. Edge-face contact shear damping factor. Edge-edge contact normal damping factor. Edge-edge contact shear damping factor. Viscous damping is specified for the blocks. See below for further information. Use local damping set to f (the damping value is 0.8 by default). f(d1) f(f1) … peak damping ratio and frequency for Maxwell element 1 f(d2) f(f2) … peak damping ratio and frequency for Maxwell element 2 f(d3) f(f3) … peak damping ratio and frequency for Maxwell element 3 zone-only b1 applies damping only to zones in range, not to contacts; default: off contact-only b2 applies damping only to contacts in range, not to zones; default: off This form of the command is normally used for dynamic calculations when a certain fraction of critical damping is required over a given frequency range. This type of damping is known as Rayleigh damping, where f1 = the fraction of critical damping operating at the center frequency of f2 . See below for further discussion. Restrict to damping to mass-proportional only. Restrict the damping to stiffness-proportional only.

keywords.name: timestep-update
keywords.description: Set interval (in cycles) for recalculating the timestep. The default is 100.
